mission.title: meshery: [Docs] Copy-to-clipboard for CLI commands
mission.description: [Docs] Copy-to-clipboard for CLI commands. Community-requested feature.

steps.title: Check current meshery deployment
steps.description:
Verify your meshery version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n meshery -l app.kubernetes.io/name=meshery
helm list -n meshery 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working meshery installation.

steps.title: Review meshery configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant meshery configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n meshery -l app.kubernetes.io/name=meshery
kubectl get configmap -n meshery -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=meshery
```
**Current State:**
CLI commands included in the Meshery documentation have to be highlighted and copied by the reader. Sometimes the reader will accidentally include the `$` in what they copy and paste to their terminal.

steps.title: Apply the fix for [Docs] Copy-to-clipboard for CLI commands
steps.description:
Yes,  you are right. Sorry, I didn't check the link properly which you shared in the previous comment. I don't think we want to do the change in the markdown file, but the structure of the site is in a way that makes it a bit difficult to workaround. 
So, you can check if we could do that like adding a separate file or we will have to do something in the markdown itself.

See the source issue for community-verified solutions.

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n meshery -l app.kubernetes.io/name=meshery
kubectl get events -n meshery --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "[Docs] Copy-to-clipboard for CLI commands" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary:
Yes,  you are right. Sorry, I didn't check the link properly which you shared in the previous comment. I don't think we want to do the change in the markdown file, but the structure of the site is in a way that makes it a bit difficult to workaround. 
So, you can check if we could do that like adding a separate file or we will have to do something in the markdown itself.

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with meshery installed or the issue environment reproducible.
